author: @VeniceInventors
text:
Thank you for having a go at it! It's awesome, especially with the light and replaceable ornaments. 
It also brings an opportunity for parents to help their kids make new ornaments before each holiday, encouraging the creative side and becoming precious moments to remember fondly.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
Thank you very much  Robert!! 
 These are absolutely Wonderful ideas for Gifts!!
I do enjoy making cards and prezzies myself, I think theyre more personal and I also prefer recieving similar myself!!

 Also,,, when we are  upcycling/recycling and repurposing,,,as well as making these ourselves,,,
 it means that  we are all  helping to curb mass production of stuff  thats going to be forgotten and discarded!!
Gifts made by our loved ones are much more likely to be treasured and appreciated!!


( Sentimental fool that I am...) 
Gratefully appreciate your kindness and generosity,~ sharing your ideas and enthusiasm!!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I fully agree - my sister always makes our gifts herself too!

author: @ezone913
text:
This type of project is where an IDEX 3D printer (Independent Dual Extruder) and conductive filaments would shine brightest. 
 Imagine printing the conductors into/onto the project. Peripherals plug in like a breadboard. Print your own circuit boards. 🤔
See if they'll send you one of those printers. 😉
Nice build, Robert.  Thanks for sharing. -EZ

author: @falcychead8198
text: You could make the moon stationary and put a shade/reflector inside to simulate the phases of the moon.  With a motor of the right speed, it could match the real phases of the moon.

author: @frostyfrances4700
text: Even though I stink at making anything more complicated than grade-school crafts, I love to watch your videos.  You explain things so well - just as Isaac Asimov did with mathematics -  that I'm left with a warm feeling that maybe I *could* if I were rash enough to make a run at it.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: do it! make a run at it - the hardest step is the first mate
